description_long: This is a small, metallic airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a single propeller mounted at the nose. The wings are straight and positioned low on the fuselage, with visible struts for support. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a clear canopy. The landing gear is fixed and consists of two main wheels and a smaller tail wheel, suitable for landing on paved or unpaved surfaces. The overall appearance suggests a light utility or training aircraft, commonly seen in urban airfields or small airports.
description: A small, metallic, single-propeller airplane with low-mounted straight wings, a conventional tail, and fixed landing gear. The cockpit is enclosed and positioned at the front of the fuselage.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, low-mounted wings, and the propeller at the nose.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the straight wings, cockpit canopy, and the tail section.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the propeller, wing struts, and the fixed landing gear.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the propeller, cockpit canopy, and the symmetry of the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight wings and visible support struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a single tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy, likely single or dual seat.
